description: Lifecycle contracts for Claude OS: install, init, update, repair, rollback, uninstall, and project bootstrap/update. Each command declares writes, backups, idempotency, rollback, and post-execution validation.

commands.title: Global install to ~/.claude (Windows PowerShell)
commands.writes: Copies CLAUDE.md, policies/*.md, prompts/*.md, optional heuristics/*.md into the target tree (default %USERPROFILE%\.claude); writes os-install.json with provenance (paths, optional git SHA). Uses Copy-Item -Force on existing same-path files.
commands.backups: No automatic snapshot. Installer does not delete arbitrary paths; risk is overwriting prior copies under the same relative paths. Archive ~/.claude before replacing a customized layout.
commands.idempotency: Documented safe to re-run: directory creation and file copies only; os-install.json is rewritten each successful non-dry run.
commands.rollbackBehavior: Restore a prior ~/.claude backup or delete the subtree and reinstall from a known-good OS repo revision; compare os-install.json sourceSha when auditing drift.
commands.validationAfterExecution: Open ~/.claude/os-install.json; from the OS repo run pwsh ./tools/verify-os-health.ps1; confirm a new agent session loads global CLAUDE.md and policies.

commands.title: Global install to ~/.claude (Unix bash)
commands.writes: Same logical layout as install.ps1: copies CLAUDE.md, policies, prompts, heuristics into $HOME/.claude (or --target); writes os-install.json with provenance when not dry-run.
commands.backups: No automatic snapshot; same overwrite semantics as Windows install for identical destination paths.
commands.idempotency: Safe to re-run per install.sh header (copies only, no deletes of unrelated files).
commands.rollbackBehavior: Restore tarball/backup of ~/.claude or remove tree and reinstall from pinned OS revision.
commands.validationAfterExecution: Inspect ~/.claude/os-install.json; run pwsh ./tools/verify-os-health.ps1 from OS clone when pwsh is available; verify global policy files present.

commands.title: Idempotent local OS checkout init
commands.writes: Creates OS_WORKSPACE_CONTEXT.md and OPERATOR_JOURNAL.md from templates when missing; creates logs/ when missing; invokes sync-agent-adapters.ps1 (may touch generated adapter paths under templates/adapters per that script). Optional write-validation-history.ps1 when -WriteHistory.
commands.backups: No automatic file backup; templates remain canonical. Operators may copy OS_WORKSPACE_CONTEXT.md before first edit if treating it as sensitive notes.
commands.idempotency: Existing OS_WORKSPACE_CONTEXT.md, OPERATOR_JOURNAL.md, and logs/ are left unchanged; safe to re-run for workspace repair.
commands.rollbackBehavior: Delete generated local-only files listed in .gitignore (e.g. OS_WORKSPACE_CONTEXT.md, OPERATOR_JOURNAL.md) and re-run init; revert adapter files via git when sync-agent-adapters modified tracked outputs.
commands.validationAfterExecution: Default path runs os-doctor (or verify-os-health with -FullHealth); expect envelope ok/warn per environment; run pwsh ./tools/verify-git-hygiene.ps1 -Json before commit when adapters changed.

commands.title: Update OS repository from upstream (git)
commands.entrypoint: Follow GIT-RECOVERY.md (fetch first, rebase or merge, resolve conflicts); optional pwsh ./tools/git-recover-local.ps1 -Mode pull after stash
commands.writes: Git working tree and refs (.git/objects, refs); may modify tracked files after merge/rebase; git-recover-local -Mode stash writes stash entry only.
commands.backups: Git history is implicit backup; use branches or tags before risky rebases; stash is a stack backup for uncommitted work per git semantics.
commands.idempotency: Not idempotent overall: repeated pull changes HEAD; stash/pull sequence should be deliberate. git-recover-local -Mode validate is read-only.
commands.rollbackBehavior: git reset --hard/reflog or merge --abort / rebase --abort per GIT-RECOVERY.md; restore stashes with git stash pop when appropriate.
commands.validationAfterExecution: pwsh ./tools/verify-git-hygiene.ps1 -Json; pwsh ./tools/os-validate.ps1 -Profile quick -Json; run pwsh ./tools/sync-agent-adapters.ps1 -Json if templates/adapters changed.

commands.title: Local Git repair helper (status, stash, pull, validate)
commands.writes: -Mode stash: creates git stash; -Mode pull: updates refs and working tree from remote; other modes read-only aside from git metadata during pull.
commands.backups: Stash preserves working tree snapshot before pull; no extra OS-level backup files created.
commands.idempotency: status and validate are read-only; stash creates new stash entries if invoked repeatedly; pull is not blindly idempotent if remote moves.
commands.rollbackBehavior: Use git stash pop/drop and reflog per GIT-RECOVERY.md; abort in-flight merge/rebase with documented commands.
commands.validationAfterExecution: pwsh ./tools/git-recover-local.ps1 -Mode validate; pwsh ./tools/verify-git-hygiene.ps1 -Json; then pwsh ./tools/os-validate.ps1 -Profile quick -Json.

commands.title: Rollback and recovery policy (human-gated)
commands.entrypoint: Read GIT-RECOVERY.md and docs/GIT-RECOVERY.md; use git revert/reset/reflog and branch protection practices as appropriate
commands.writes: None by reading the policy; executing documented git commands changes repository state per chosen command.
commands.backups: Relies on git objects, stashes, tags, and external backups; policy describes fetch-first and abort paths.
commands.idempotency: Policy document is static; recovery commands are situational, not universally idempotent.
commands.rollbackBehavior: Policy itself is guidance; undo depends on chosen git command (e.g. reset --hard vs revert) and is documented in GIT-RECOVERY.md.
commands.validationAfterExecution: pwsh ./tools/verify-git-hygiene.ps1 -Json -WarnIfNoGit; pwsh ./tools/os-validate.ps1 -Profile standard -Json when tree should be clean for merge.

commands.title: Remove global ~/.claude install (manual)
commands.entrypoint: Close agents using global files; archive or delete %USERPROFILE%\.claude (Unix: $HOME/.claude) after confirming no other tools share that directory
commands.writes: Manual deletion or archival of the target directory; no bundled uninstall.ps1.
commands.backups: Operator should zip or rename ~/.claude before deletion if any custom policies or prompts must be preserved.
commands.idempotency: Deleting a missing directory is a no-op; partial deletes are not automatically reconciled.
commands.rollbackBehavior: Restore from backup archive; re-run install.ps1 / install.sh from a known OS revision.
commands.validationAfterExecution: Confirm directory absent or empty as intended; start a new shell and verify agents no longer load removed global policies.

commands.title: Remove project-local Claude OS artifacts (manual)
commands.entrypoint: Delete or archive <ProjectPath>/.claude/, project adapter copies (AGENTS.md, .cursor/rules/*, .agent/*, .agents/* per project policy), and optional .claudeignore if project-owned
commands.writes: Manual filesystem changes only; do not delete application source outside declared OS artifact paths.
commands.backups: Archive .claude/ and tracked adapter files before removal if the project customized them beyond OS defaults.
commands.idempotency: Removing already-absent paths is a no-op; mixed manual steps require operator discipline.
commands.rollbackBehavior: Restore archive or re-run pwsh ./tools/os-runtime.ps1 bootstrap -ProjectPath <path> then pwsh ./tools/os-update-project.ps1 -ProjectPath <path> from a pinned OS clone.
commands.validationAfterExecution: Run application tests; confirm pwsh ./tools/query-docs-index.ps1 (if copied) missing or intentionally removed; verify no stale OS paths in CI.

commands.title: Bootstrap a new application repo with full .claude tree (Windows)
commands.writes: Creates/updates .claude/** managed tree, templates-derived commands/agents/skills/policies, adapter files (AGENTS.md, .cursor, .agent, .agents per profile), optional git init; -Force refreshes selected managed markdown from templates.
commands.backups: No automatic full-project backup; use git status before run; -DryRun lists mutations. Archive existing .claude/ before destructive manual cleanup.
commands.idempotency: Re-runnable with stable managed paths; project-owned files skipped unless -Force for declared managed markdown; adapter template files refreshed per init-project rules.
commands.rollbackBehavior: git checkout -- paths or restore from branch; remove .claude/ if first bootstrap mistaken; document human approval for destructive reversions per CLAUDE.md.
commands.validationAfterExecution: pwsh ./tools/verify-bootstrap-manifest.ps1; pwsh ./tools/verify-bootstrap-examples.ps1; from project root run pwsh .claude/scripts/query-docs-index.ps1 -Query bootstrap when copied; optional pwsh ./tools/os-validate.ps1 -Profile quick -Json from OS repo.

commands.title: Refresh managed Claude OS files in an existing project
commands.writes: Overwrites managed manifests and scripts under <ProjectPath>/.claude per allowlist; copies skills and policies directories; creates .claudeignore from template only if missing (project-owned thereafter).
commands.backups: No automatic per-file backup; operator should commit or snapshot .claude/ before update when drift review matters.
commands.idempotency: Re-running applies same copy rules; project-owned .claudeignore and existing project-only files outside managed set are preserved per script invariants.
commands.rollbackBehavior: git revert on tracked copies under app repo or restore branch; re-copy from previous OS export if OS-side only.
commands.validationAfterExecution: pwsh ./tools/verify-json-contracts.ps1 from OS repo; open updated manifests for diff review; run project CI; optionally pwsh ./tools/query-docs-index.ps1 -Query bootstrap using project-local script.
